title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed all four of Cleveland's runs in the first inning during the Red Sox's 4-3 loss.
Brayan Bello excelled in bulk relief, allowing no runs or walks and striking out five in seven innings.
The Red Sox face challenges with opener strategy and are 10 games under .500 after losing to the Guardians.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics fans and the TD Garden, highlighting Boston as a passionate place to play.
Boston and Miami Heat are both contenders to acquire Antetokounmpo, though Miami is considered the frontrunner.
Speculation includes a potential trade involving Jaylen Brown, with Celtics examining roster fit challenges with Antetokounmpo.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police cleared Harvard's Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd concerns and swimming attempts.
The gathering involved hundreds from Harvard's Class of 2026, shortly after their commencement ceremonies.
Massachusetts State Police and other agencies responded to the blocked pathway and potential river swimming attempts.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is now available for streaming on Peacock.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2 featuring Tina Fey and Will Forte is streaming on Netflix.
'Star City', a spin-off from 'For All Mankind', is streaming on Apple TV+.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox projected to draft Bo Lowrance, a third baseman from South Carolina, in ESPN's 2026 MLB mock draft.
Bo Lowrance, 18, bats left-handed and is committed to the University of Virginia. He stands 6-foot-5 and weighs 200 pounds.
Comparisons to Freddie Freeman are drawn, but Lowrance's future position could shift from third to first base.

title: Lowell father and daughter charged in alleged payroll tax scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lim Ou, 69, and Nalen Ou, 39, of Lowell, charged with concealing over $48 million in cash payroll from the IRS.
Prosecutors allege the Ous ran staffing agencies since 2013, underpaying taxes by over $12 million through unreported cash wages.
The tax scheme involved using a Worcester check-cashing business to pay workers without reporting wages or paying employment taxes.

title: Report: Christian Gonzalez unlikely to participate in OTAs under current Patriots contract
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez skipped Patriots OTAs due to ongoing contract extension negotiations.
Gonzalez seeks an extension above his $4 million rookie contract, aiming for $32-35 million annually.
Patriots created cap space by restructuring Mike Onwenu's contract, aiding potential Gonzalez extension.

title: Search underway for driver who led cops on a chase through Everett, Malden, Stoneham
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Suspect led police on a chase through Everett, Malden, and Stoneham, striking a State Police cruiser.
The driver, wanted on drug warrants, was pursued by police before abandoning the vehicle in Stoneham.
Police are searching for the suspect and a passenger, who fled on foot after leaving the car.

title: Ex-Patriots HC Jerod Mayo has a new job away from football
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jerod Mayo joined Fifth Down Capital as managing director in February after being fired as Patriots head coach.
Fifth Down Capital, a private equity firm based in Needham, invests in technology, healthcare, and consumer sectors.
Mayo, 40, was previously Optum's VP of Business Development and led the Patriots to a 4-13 season in 2024.

title: Mass. man reports beating Mount Everest speed climbing record
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Andrews from Massachusetts reportedly set a new speed record for climbing Mount Everest in 9 hours and 55 minutes.
The previous record of 10 hours and 56 minutes was held by Nepali climber Lhakpa Gelu Sherpa since 2003.
Andrews' climb was the seventh attempt, marking his dedication despite previous setbacks such as bad weather and equipment issues.
